notes: Six Visitors. Three on board at game start, three arrive on schedule. The DEFAULT connection path is the four-card waiter sequence: GREET → LISTEN → DELIVER → SIT WITH. Some visitors have a SPECIAL bypass path (composite or auto-trigger). Each visitor has a `mood` (jovial / gruff / preoccupied / left_alone) and per-step flavor lines so the same mechanical sequence reads as a real conversation per character.

visitors.name: Frasier Temple
visitors.lore_text: Still chasing shadows. Frasier sees you, you see Frasier.
pre_arrival_hints: Voices murmur somewhere past the kitchen door.
Footsteps in the alcove — someone is waiting to come through.
The kitchen door swings. Someone is about to step out.

steps.greet: Frasier looks up from his notebook just long enough to register that you exist. He goes back to writing.
steps.listen: He tells you about the model. The river isn't right. The whole thing won't hold unless the river is right. "And a coffee. And the toast." You nod.
steps.deliver: You set the coffee and toast in front of him. He pulls them close without looking up. Half the coffee gone in one swallow.
steps.sit_with: Frasier sets his notebook on the counter and turns it toward you. The handwriting is yours, except where it isn't. He nods, like you've finally caught up to him.

visitors.name: The Booth-Six Stranger
visitors.lore_text: You were seen first. There was an observer before the deck.
visitors.tutorial_note: The stranger doesn't want to be sat with. Pick up the cloth from their booth and visit the CARD WALL — they connect through what you take to heart, not what you bring them.

steps.greet: They don't look up. The glow of their handheld screen washes their hood in green.
steps.listen: Nothing. The screen they're reading from is the only voice in the booth.
steps.deliver: You set down a coffee. It cools untouched.
steps.sit_with: The stranger does not lift their eyes. SIT WITH won't connect them — try the booth's cloth and the card wall.

visitors.name: Faith
visitors.lore_text: The dog's name is Faith. She is also the mandatory leap witness.
visitors.tutorial_note: Faith is a dog. Use CALL FAITH twice.

steps.greet: Faith thumps her tail twice. Once for hello. Once for you.
steps.listen: She presses her nose against your shin. The pressure means: yes.
steps.deliver: A piece of jerky from the back room. She accepts it slowly, like it's owed.
steps.sit_with: Faith doesn't sit with — she stands up. Try CALL FAITH again.

visitors.name: The Dawn Cook
visitors.lore_text: Shift change. The next person who'll wipe this counter.
pre_arrival_hints: Headlights swing across the parking lot — a truck idles.
A car door slams. Someone's outside smoking by the dumpster.
Boots crunch the gravel. The back door rattles in its frame.

steps.greet: The cook grunts once, half-recognizes you, half doesn't. They're already tying the apron.
steps.listen: "Skillet. Cajun. Hash on the side. Eggs." They say it like they're listing weather.
steps.deliver: You set the skillet down. The cook nods at the andouille rounds, satisfied, doesn't say thanks.
steps.sit_with: The cook leans against the counter beside you. Wordless. The apron is on. The counter is taken care of.

visitors.name: The BBS Caller
visitors.lore_text: sysop ember.ash.rest.bbs — the network has been counting the dead all night.
visitors.placeholder_name: an unread line on the BBS
pre_arrival_hints: The BBS terminal blinks an unread line in the corner.
The modem chirps. A connection is opening.
Text scrolls fast on the register screen. Someone is typing now.

steps.greet: The cursor on the register screen pulses. A line types itself: HELLO, JOHN.
steps.listen: The caller wants grits, eggs over hard, gravy. They also drop a TO-GO ticket on the BBS — chicken and waffles for whoever's in the parking lot.
steps.deliver: You set down the grits, eggs, gravy. The terminal acknowledges with a single clean line of green.
steps.sit_with: WE SEE YOU. RETURN AT YOUR DISCRETION. The cursor blinks twice and waits.

visitors.name: The Bus Kid
visitors.notes: Helper visitor. Doesn't need to be connected — they're staff. While they're on the board, threats decay one tick faster per Upkeep in any space adjacent to them (engine support coming). Disperse / claim effects skip helpers.

steps.greet: They wave a wet rag at you and grin.
steps.listen: "On it," they say, before you've even said what.
steps.deliver: They take the plate from your tray as it passes them.
steps.sit_with: They take a knee on a bus tub, stretch their back, breathe.

visitors.name: The Line Cook
visitors.notes: Helper visitor. Auto-rings the order bell each Upkeep regardless of player position (engine support coming).

steps.greet: They give you the look. The one that says go work.
steps.listen: "You want it the right way or the fast way."
steps.deliver: They slide the plate across the pass without looking up.
steps.sit_with: They lean on the cold side of the grill and roll a cigarette they won't light.

visitors.name: Anya's Recording
visitors.lore_text: The Priestess is older than the chapter. Elicia has been recording you for years.
visitors.tutorial_note: Anya connects automatically when you BUNDLE with the cassette spine as contents.

steps.greet: The cassette spins one full reel.
steps.listen: A woman's voice you almost remember says your name.
steps.deliver: She names the year. It was before you started here.
steps.sit_with: The tape ends. The spool clicks. The recording was already finished.

visitors.name: Nicola
visitors.lore_text: Nicola comes for the grits, and stays for the bench seat near the window. She has been coming here since before you started.
pre_arrival_hints: The door chimes. Someone takes the front bench like it's theirs.
She's setting her bag down at the hostess stand.
Nicola, smiling like she's already glad to see you.

steps.greet: She lifts her sunglasses onto her hair. "There he is. Hi, doll."
steps.listen: "Spaghetti special. Meatballs. Extra parm. Don't tell my doctor. And the pomodoro to-go for my granddaughter — she's at the door already."
steps.deliver: She closes her eyes when the plate lands. "Better than yesterday. You know it's better than yesterday."
steps.sit_with: She tells you how her granddaughter's doing. The names of three teachers. The plate is empty when you stand up.

visitors.name: Mackenzie
visitors.lore_text: She made the call from your phone. You only ever heard her side of it. The voice on the other end was somebody who used to know you, too.
pre_arrival_hints: The door. Someone walks straight past the booths to the back.
She's looking at the pay phone like it owes her.
Mackenzie, lifting the receiver, already dialing.

steps.greet: She holds up one finger to you. The call comes first.
steps.listen: Gumbo, mouthed at you over the receiver. Two fingers up: crabclaw. She points at the bowl on the menu, then mimes one to go for the hostess stand. "My friend's outside."
steps.deliver: She catches the bowl-edge with two fingers while still on the call. "You're a saint. No, not you."
steps.sit_with: She sets the receiver down very carefully. "I just told him," she says, to nobody. Then to you: "Thanks."

visitors.name: Maya
visitors.lore_text: She showed you a card trick. She showed you a card trick. You watched it three times. You did not see the card move.

steps.greet: She slides a deck across the booth toward you. "Pick one. Don't say it."
steps.listen: She tells you about the trick. Then about her uncle. Then about the trick again, and you realize she's been doing it the whole time.
steps.deliver: She hands the card back to you face-up. It was your card. You don't remember telling her.
steps.sit_with: She grins. Her parents look up from their plates. She winks at you. The deck goes back into her sleeve in one motion.

visitors.name: Elicia
visitors.lore_text: She comes in late. She sits in Frasier's old booth. She presses record. She never tells you when she starts.
pre_arrival_hints: A small figure at the door, watching the room without entering yet.
She's choosing a booth. The one in the back.
Elicia, easing into Booth 4 with a cassette recorder on the table.

steps.greet: She nods up at you and clicks the record button without looking. She says: "Whenever you're ready."
steps.listen: Pappardelle, the seafood. Coffee, black. She wants to know about the night Frasier last sat where she's sitting. She lets you take your time.
steps.deliver: She thanks the food the way you thank a witness. The reels keep turning.
steps.sit_with: She stops the tape. "That's the one," she says. She doesn't say one of what. You know.

visitors.name: the man at the bar
visitors.lore_text: He sat where the bar would be, if the bar were open. He was already drinking. He'd been waiting an hour. He left a tip on a check you never gave him.
pre_arrival_hints: The door doesn't ring when he comes in.
He's at the bar already. You don't remember serving him.
He raises two fingers at you without looking up.
visitors.notes: The Devil cameo — his presence makes the room's claim ticks one space closer. Engine support for this aura coming later.

steps.greet: He doesn't look up. "You know me."
steps.listen: "Same as last time." You don't remember last time. The bisque. The bowl. "And the spoon."
steps.deliver: The bowl is cold before you set it down. He doesn't eat it. He nods anyway.
steps.sit_with: He turns his head, finally, and the look is not unkind. "I won't be here long," he tells you. The stool beside him cools.

visitors.name: Alberto
visitors.lore_text: Dante's elder son. The man who would not sleep after the night his father died. He came back to the diner like the diner could explain.
pre_arrival_hints: A car door in the lot. The bell. Slow steps.
He's at booth 1, still in his coat. He hasn't taken off his sunglasses.
Alberto, sliding the menu away without opening it.

steps.greet: He doesn't look up. Doesn't move. You set the menu down anyway.
steps.listen: He orders without lifting his head. "Filet. Lobster. The way he liked it." Then, quieter: "And the pork chop, to go. Burrell. My brother's on his way over." You write it down.
steps.deliver: The plate sits between you for a long moment before he touches it. "Thanks," he says, to the formica.
steps.sit_with: You stay. He picks up the fork. He sets it down. "He used to sit at the counter," he says, finally. "I never sat with him." You nod.

visitors.name: John Frank (the regular)
visitors.lore_text: Before you worked here, you came here. Twelve years of coming first, then twelve years of staying.
pre_arrival_hints: The bell over the door. A familiar pace across the linoleum.
Same stool as always.
The face in the mirror behind the counter is your own, twelve years younger.
visitors.notes: Stand-in visitor used when the player's HAND is John Frank's and a scenario would otherwise put John on the board as a visitor (a future configuration). Currently inert because only john_frank hand exists; documented for the future hand additions.

steps.greet: He nods at you. You nod back. You used to sit in that seat too.
steps.listen: He orders without speaking. You know what it is.
steps.deliver: The plate lands. He cups the mug and breathes the steam.
steps.sit_with: He sits with himself. You sit with him. The diner is between you.

visitors.name: Frasier Temple (the regular)
visitors.lore_text: A stand-in for when the player is Frasier and the engine would otherwise spawn Frasier as a visitor.
visitors.notes: Stand-in used when the player's HAND is Frasier's.

steps.greet: He looks up from a notebook the same way you do.
steps.listen: He orders coffee. The toast is implied.
steps.deliver: The plate lands. He nods once.
steps.sit_with: He sits with himself. You let him be.

jovial.deliver: You give them what they asked for. They thank you.
jovial.sit_with: They share the silence with you.

gruff.listen: They tell you in three words.
gruff.deliver: You do the thing. They accept it like it's owed.
gruff.sit_with: They sit. The silence is theirs.

preoccupied.listen: They tell you sideways, as if mostly to themselves.
preoccupied.deliver: You give them what they asked for. They take it without looking.
preoccupied.sit_with: They sit. The silence is a long held breath.

left_alone.deliver: You set it down. They don't touch it.
left_alone.sit_with: They do not lift their eyes. This may not be the path.
